message:
feat(pager): skip flusher arrival wait when batches pre-queued, break commit-path lock convoy

Two interrelated performance improvements to the group commit subsystem:

1. Flusher loop arrival-wait bypass:
   - The first-iteration wait logic now checks pending_batch_count() > 1
     or should_flush_now() BEFORE entering the spin-wait loop. When
     pipelined batches have already accumulated (epoch pipelining), the
     redundant wait is skipped entirely.
   - Spin-wait deadline reduced from 50 microseconds to 20 microseconds.
   - Eliminates the "flushing_wait bottleneck" on pipelined workloads
     identified in the epoch pipelining commit (a17ba22a).

2. Commit-path lock convoy elimination:
   - Split the commit success path into two phases:
     Phase C1 (under inner.lock): metadata update + file unlock only
     Phase C2 (after drop(inner)): publish_committed_state() runs
     OUTSIDE inner.lock
   - Previously publish_committed_state() was called while holding
     inner.lock, causing 16 threads to serialize on both inner.lock
     and publish_lock simultaneously (lock convoy). The two locks are
     now never held together, allowing concurrent commit metadata
     updates and state publication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
